items.title: β-arrestin1 orchestrates endosomal signaling to regulate translational control of circadian light entrainment
items.author: Hai-Ying Mary Cheng
items.content: β arrestin1-mediated endosomal GPCR signaling is shown to be a crucial component of light-induced entrainment of the central circadian clock in mammals.

items.title: Mini-bacterioferritins: structural insight into a ferritin-like protein from the anaerobic methane-oxidising archaeon Candidatus Methanoperedens carboxydivorans
items.author: Tristan Wagner
items.content: Ferritin-like proteins from the anaerobic methane-oxidizing archaeon ‘Candidatus Methanoperedens carboxydivorans’ assemble as a 12-subunit complex incorporating six heme cofactors, expanding our view of ferritin structure and evolution.

items.title: 3D morphology of the Cambrian bivalved arthropod Sunella informs about head segmentation, arthrodization, and arthropodization
items.author: Xingliang Zhang
items.content: Three-dimensional preservation of the Cambrian arthropod Sunella reveals new details of head segmentation and limb organisation, informing early stages of arthropod body-plan evolution.

items.title: Neuronal ARHGAP8 controls synapse structure and AMPA receptor-mediated synaptic transmission
items.author: Ana Luisa Carvalho
items.content: ARHGAP8 is identified as an excitatory synaptic protein linked to neurodevelopmental disorders. Increased ARHGAP8 levels reduce dendritic complexity, spine size, and AMPA receptor-mediated signalling, highlighting a role in synapse function.

items.title: Linguistic structure and language familiarity sharpen phoneme encoding in the brain
items.author: Andrea E. Martin
items.content: Phoneme encoding was stronger for sentences than word lists, and within words than random syllables. Similar encoding appeared for an uncomprehended language only after prior exposure. Acoustic edges were briefly suppressed early in comprehension.

items.title: Early life chronic stress-disrupted activity of the dorsal raphe nucleus selectively drives behavioral impairments
items.author: Florence Kermen
items.content: Disrupted dorsal raphe activity following early-life stress selectively impairs startle response habituation, despite broader behavioural consequences.

items.title: Ontogeny independent expression of LPCAT2 in granuloma macrophages during experimental visceral leishmaniasis
items.author: Paul M. Kaye
items.content: Multimodal spatial imaging of Leishmania donovani-induced hepatic granulomas reveals LPCAT2-mediated lipid membrane remodeling in myeloid cells as a key immunometabolic feature of granuloma formation and macrophage activation.

items.title: The redundant protein synthesis gene Aimp1 challenges the canonical inverse relationship between translation and autophagy
items.author: Margaret A. Schwarz
items.content: A genomic approach predicts an exception to the inverse relationship between protein synthesis and autophagy unlinked from mTOR, which is validated both in vitro and in vivo.
